narrative_summary:
The BRCA1 p.Arg7Cys (R7C) missense change (c.19C>T) is a rare variant located in the N‑terminal region of the BRCA1 protein and has been reported in breast and ovarian cancer cohorts from several ethnic groups [21918854][PMC3725882].
Clinical observations are heterogeneous. The variant was identified among a panel of BRCA1/2 mutations in Iranian high‑risk families but no pathogenicity assignment was made [21918854]. An Argentinean series also listed it as a rare missense change with limited clinical data [PMC3725882]. In a large unselected Chinese Han breast‑cancer cohort the variant was re‑classified as benign based on case‑control frequencies [PMC7814423]. Norwegian diagnostic laboratories have assigned conflicting classes ranging from likely benign (class 2) to variant of uncertain significance (class 3), reflecting interpretive uncertainty [PMC9636114]. An ENIGMA multifactorial likelihood analysis and a separate ClinVar‑derived meta‑predictor study both concluded that the evidence favours a benign interpretation, citing lack of functional impact and moderate benign evidence [PMC6772163][PMC6726608]. Conversely, a spatial heterogeneity study of oesophageal squamous cell carcinoma reported the variant as a driver mutation in a metastatic subregion, suggesting possible pathogenic relevance in that context [PMC10679972].
Functional assays provide no support for a deleterious effect. The ENIGMA resource reported no detectable impact on BRCA1 protein function in the Findlay saturation genome editing assay, classifying the variant as having no functional impact [PMC6772163]. Blood‑derived RNA sequencing showed normal splicing and no splice‑disruption for the c.19C>T change, further indicating a lack of functional consequence at the transcript level [PMC7272326].
Population data show the allele is extremely rare, with a highest minor allele frequency below 0.0001 in non‑Finnish European gnomAD samples and no enrichment in cancer cases, consistent with a low‑frequency benign variant [PMC6772163].
Computational predictions are discordant. SIFT and GVGD analyses gave a relatively low deleteriousness score for Arg7Cys, suggesting limited impact on protein structure, whereas a structural deviation analysis in an Asian cohort predicted a high deleteriousness score (33 % deviation) and classified the variant as deleterious [PMC3725882][PMC10098510]. Meta‑predictor aggregations (REVEL, BayesDel) and other in‑silico tools largely supported a benign classification, aligning with the clinical re‑classifications [PMC6726608].
Overall, the preponderance of clinical re‑classifications, functional assays, and population frequency data support a benign or likely benign interpretation for BRCA1 Arg7Cys, although isolated reports of pathogenicity in specific tumor contexts and some computational models predicting deleteriousness introduce residual uncertainty. The current consensus leans toward the variant being non‑pathogenic, but further functional validation in relevant tissue models would be needed to resolve the remaining discordance.

mentions.quoted_text: Breast cancer is the most common cancer in Iranian women (Mousavi et al in Asian Pac J Cancer Prev 9(2):275-278, 2008). Genetic predisposition accounts for 15% of all breast cancers and germline mutations in breast cancer susceptibility genes, BRCA1 and BRCA2 are responsible for a substantial proportion of high-risk breast and breast/ovarian cancer families (Collaborative Group on Hormonal Factors in Breast Cancer in Lancet 350:1047-1059, 1997; Lee et al in Int Nurs Rev 55:355-359, 2008; Hulka and Stark in Lancet 346:883-887, 1995; Kelsey in Epidemiol Rev 15:256-263, 1993; Tischer et al in J Biol Chem 266:11947-11954, 1991; Newman et al in: Proc Natl Acad Sci USA 85:3044-3048, 1988). Therefore, the aim of this study was to investigate mutations of BRCA1/2 in high risk Iranian families. We screened 85 patients who met our minimal criteria. The entire coding sequences and each intron/exon boundaries of BRCA1/2 genes were screened by direct sequencing. In the present study, we could detect the novel following mutations: p.Glu1735 p.Gly1140Ser, p.Ile26Val, p.Leu1418X, p.Glu23Gln, p.Leu3X, p.Asn1403His, p.Lys581X, p.Pro938Arg, p.Thr77Arg, **p.Arg7Cys**, p.Ser177Thr, IVS7+83(TT), IVS8-70(-CATT), IVS2+9(-GC), IVS1-20(-GA), IVS1-8(-AG), IVS2+24(AG), IVS5-8 (A-G), IVS2(35-39)TTcctatGAT in BRCA1 and p.Glu1391Gly, 1994_1995 (Ins A), IVS6-70-T>G in BRCA2. In agreement with findings in other populations, we found that family history is a good predictor of being a mutation carrier. Five pathogenic BRCA1 mutations and one pathogenic BRCA2 mutation were detected in 85 index cases.

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study screened 85 Iranian breast cancer patients and reported the detection of the BRCA1 p.Arg7Cys variant among other mutations, without assigning a specific pathogenicity classification to it.
article_mentions.title: BRCA1 and BRCA2 germline mutations in 85 Iranian breast cancer patients.

Cys61Gly c.181 T > G D 1 (1.1) - NT C65 - p. Arg71Gly c.211A > G D 1 (1.1) - NT C65 - p. Val122Asp c.365 T > A NR 5 (5.3) BRCA2 T C0 - p. Gln139Lys c.415C > A NR 6...

Five of the 28 missense variants (Table 4) (i.e., p. **Arg7Cys**, p. Cys61Gly, p. Arg71Gly, p. Tyr179Cys, and p. Met1652Thr in BRCA1, p. Asp2723His in BRCA2) were predicted to have an impact on protein structure upon evaluation by SIFT and GVGD (Table 4). BRCA1 p. **Arg7Cys**, differently from the other non-conservative variants, has a rather low prediction score and was found in two cases. The high prediction values for BRCA1 p. Cys61Gly and BRCA1 p. Arg71gly agree with their previously reported pathogenicity (Table 4). Few reported data are available for BRCA2 p. Asp2723His. BRCA1 p. Met1652Thr, located in the BRCT tandem repeat region is predicted to result in a large volume change in rigid neighbourhood but structural and functional assays show normal peptide binding specificity and transcriptional activity. Tyr179Cys is also located in a highly conserved region and is listed as clinically importance unknown (CU) in BIC. Notably BRCA1 Tyr179Cys co-occurred with two other missense mutations, i.e., Phe486Leu and Asn550His, in an FH patient affected with pagetoid BC (AB80). These 3 mutations, already reported to occur together, may constitute a rare haplotype brca.iarc.fr/LOVD].

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study describes the spectrum of BRCA1/2 variants in Argentinean breast/ovarian cancer patients and notes that the BRCA1 p.Arg7Cys (R7C) missense change is a rare variant with low computational prediction scores and limited clinical data, leaving its significance uncertain.
article_mentions.title: BRCA1 And BRCA2 analysis of Argentinean breast/ovarian cancer patients selected for age and family history highlights a role for novel mutations of putative south-American origin

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The large Chinese breast‑cancer cohort study reports ethnicity‑specific BRCA1/2 variant frequencies and reclassifies many VUS, including classifying the BRCA1 Arg7Cys (R7C) variant as benign in its supplementary data.
article_mentions.title: Prevalence and reclassification of BRCA1 and BRCA2 variants in a large, unselected Chinese Han breast cancer cohort

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The BRCA1 p.Arg7Cys (c.19C>T) variant shows inconsistent classification among Norwegian laboratories, ranging from likely benign (class 2) to variant of uncertain significance (class 3), highlighting interpretive uncertainty.
article_mentions.title: BRCA1 Norway: comparison of classification for BRCA1 germline variants detected in families with suspected hereditary breast and ovarian cancer between different laboratories

article_mentions.title: Ethnic-specificity, evolution origin and deleteriousness of Asian BRCA variation revealed by over 7500 BRCA variants derived from Asian population

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The article presents a computational framework for predicting the pathogenicity of SNPs in genes linked to triple‑negative breast cancer, identifying BRCA1 among the recurrent genes but offering no specific interpretation for the R7C variant.
article_mentions.title: Analysis of single-nucleotide polymorphisms in genes associated with triple-negative breast cancer

article_mentions.title: Multi-omics analyses reveal spatial heterogeneity in primary and metastatic oesophageal squamous cell carcinoma

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The article evaluates in silico meta-predictors and includes a ClinVar-derived entry for BRCA1 p.R7C showing likely benign/uncertain classifications and computational evidence supporting a benign interpretation.
article_mentions.title: REVEL and BayesDel outperform other in silico meta-predictors for clinical variant classification
